topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

Liam scrolled through his feed during a particularly dull lecture on 18th-century poetry. He stopped dead when he saw a notification. It was Professor Albright, his notoriously stern literature instructor, tagging him in a photo of a surprisingly well-executed latte art creation. Liam blinked once, then twice. A slow, spreading warmth bloomed in his chest. He snorted, a small, involuntary sound that he quickly muffled behind his hand. He re-read the caption: "A little caffeine to fuel the existential dread. #AcademicLife." Liam leaned back in his chair, a genuine smile tugging at the corners of his mouth, shaking his head slightly as he considered the sheer audacity of the post.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" Maya whispered, loud enough only for her friend, Chloe, to hear. She was staring at her phone, her lips twitching upwards. Professor Davies, the notoriously reserved history teacher, had just posted a short, slightly blurry video clip of himself attempting to assemble IKEA furniture, complete with a caption about the challenges of modern pedagogy. Maya let out a soft, breathy chuckle, covering her mouth with her hand. She couldn't help but grin, the tension she’d been holding in her shoulders dissolving into a pleasant lightness. It was just… unexpected.

[story 3]

I nearly dropped my textbook. It was Professor Evans. I was doom-scrolling, trying to distract myself from the looming deadline, when I saw it: a picture of me, looking utterly bewildered while attempting to explain quantum physics to a rubber duck. The caption was a witty critique of my presentation skills. A strange, bubbly feeling washed over me. I let out a low, rumbling laugh that vibrated in my chest. I tapped the screen repeatedly, a wide, unguarded expression spreading across my face as I replayed the absurdity of the situation. It was certainly something to think about during study hall.
